title: catabrosa scabra
intro: Catabrosa scabra, also known as rough sedge or rough club-rush, is a type of aquatic plant that belongs to the family Juncaceae. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which have been found to aid in wound healing and reducing fever. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing the risk of certain infections, alleviating respiratory issues, and acting as a diuretic. Catabrosa scabra is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are consumed for its medicinal properties or applied topically to benefit from its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects.
